label: ∅ | live | queen
sentence: Once, in a flourishing realm of ancient Persia, there lived a queen blessed with a little daughter, a child still too tender to wander the palace gardens alone.

label: child | grow | ∅
sentence: One day, the child grew restless and tearful, and no matter how gently the queen tried to soothe her, the cries would not cease.

label: queen | gaze | window
sentence: Overcome with weariness, the queen gazed out the lattice window toward the dark cypress trees, where flocks of black ravens wheeled in the gilded sunlight.

label: i | find | peace
sentence: In a moment of despair, she whispered, “Oh, that my daughter might become a raven and fly away—then I would find but a moment’s peace.”

label: ∅ | have | ∅
sentence: No sooner had the words left her lips than her beloved child transformed, feathers sprouting swiftly where soft skin had been.

label: raven | flap | wing
sentence: The little raven flapped its wings and darted through the open window, vanishing into the shadowed groves beyond the palace walls.

label: queen | search | ∅
sentence: The queen and king searched far and wide but heard no word of their lost princess.

label: traveler | wander | forest
sentence: Some time later, a determined traveler wandered through the same forest when his ears caught the familiar cawing of a raven.

label: he | find | ∅
sentence: Drawn to the voice, he found the bird perched upon a low branch.

label: raven | speak | ∅
sentence: “I was born a king’s daughter,” the raven spoke softly, “but a sorcerer’s spell holds me captive in this form.

label: you | break | bondage
sentence: You alone can break my bondage.”

label: ∅ | ask | traveler
sentence: “What must I do?” asked the traveler.

label: she | offer | food
sentence: She will offer you food and wine, but refuse her gifts, lest you fall into a cursed slumber and fail to free me.

label: you | stand | ∅
sentence: In the garden behind her house is a great pile of fragrant cedar leaves; on that you must stand watch.

label: i | come | day
sentence: For three days, each at the stroke of afternoon, I shall come in my royal carriage.

label: freedom | be | yours
sentence: If you sleep while I arrive, my freedom will never be yours to claim.”

label: traveler | swear | ∅
sentence: The traveler swore he would heed her warning, but the raven’s eyes clouded with doubt.

label: i | fear | promise
sentence:
“Alas, I fear you will falter, despite your promises.”

When he reached the dwelling and entered, the old woman welcomed him warmly.

label: you | look | ∅
sentence: “Friend, you look weary.

label: she | urge | ∅
sentence: Rest and partake of bread and sweet wine,” she urged.

label: he | say | ∅
sentence: “I cannot eat or drink,” he said firmly.

label: she | press | ∅
sentence: She pressed on until, with weakening will, he sipped the wine.

label: traveler | climb | heap
sentence: As the first appointed hour drew near, the traveler climbed upon the cedar heap, eyes fixed on the horizon.

label: fatigue | envelop | he
sentence: Fatigue enveloped him like a silk garment, and though he fought it fiercely, sleep claimed him.

label: she | sigh | ∅
sentence: When the princess came riding by her four white horses, she sighed, “He has fallen,” and found him slumbering deeply despite her calls.

label: scene | repeat | ∅
sentence: The next day, the same scene repeated.

label: traveler | resist | ∅
sentence: The traveler resisted at first but finally drank again from the old woman’s cup.

label: he | succumb | ∅
sentence: Exhausted, he succumbed to sleep as the princess approached in her carriage drawn by chestnut steeds.

label: she | find | he
sentence: She found him once more unawakened and sorrowfully left him.

label: woman | press | traveler
sentence: On the third day, the old woman pressed the traveler with even greater insistence.

label: he | drink | himself
sentence: Scenting the sweet wine, he drank despite himself.

label: he | fall | sleep
sentence: That afternoon, heavier than before, he fell into a deep sleep.

label: princess | arrive | horse
sentence: The princess arrived behind her black horses, more mournful than ever.

label: she | place | loaf
sentence: Unable to rouse him, she placed beside him an enchanted loaf of bread, smoked meats, and a flask of wine—gifts that would never diminish regardless of how much he partook.

label: she | remove | ring
sentence: From her finger she removed a ring inscribed with her name and slipped it onto his hand.

label: she | leave | letter
sentence:
Finally, she left a letter:

“As long as you remain here, you cannot release me.

label: ∅ | seek | fortress
sentence: Yet if your heart remains steadfast, seek the Golden Fortress of Shahrazad.

label: ∅ | lie | power
sentence: There lies the power you need.”

label: she | return | carriage
sentence: She returned to her carriage and vanished toward the distant Golden Fortress.

label: traveler | wash | he
sentence: When the traveler awoke, sorrow washed over him for he feared all was lost.

label: ∅ | fill | he
sentence: Yet reading the letter filled him with resolve.

label: he | set | ∅
sentence: He set out to find this fortress, journeying through shadowed forests for many days, until twilight found him too weary to go further.

label: he | sleep | tree
sentence: Under a tamarisk tree, he slept.

label: cry | stir | he
sentence: On the next dusk, strange cries stirred him—the howling of jackals and the groans of unseen spirits.

label: he | come | hut
sentence: Guided by a flickering light, he came upon a humble hut overshadowed by a giant sentinel.

label: giant | declare | ∅
sentence: The towering giant declared, “You are a fortunate guest—for I have naught to eat and hunger grows fierce.

label: you | be | feast
sentence: You shall be my feast.”

label: traveler | plead | ∅
sentence: The traveler pleaded, “Spare me.

label: i | offer | bread
sentence: If you hunger, I offer you this endless bread, wine, and meat.”

label: giant | accept | ∅
sentence: Content with such sustenance, the giant accepted and shared the meal.

label: traveler | inquire | fortress
sentence: Afterwards, the traveler inquired about the Golden Fortress.

label: giant | search | scroll
sentence: The giant searched his scrolls and maps but found none that named it.

label: they | wait | brother
sentence: They waited for the giant’s brother, who returned with new charts.

label: fortress | lie | ∅
sentence: Still, the fortress lay unmarked—hidden far, far away.

label: giant | offer | ∅
sentence: Finally, the giant offered to carry the traveler near the fortress before he had to return to care for a child in his keeping.

label: traveler | set | mountain
sentence: The traveler was set down near the distant mountain on which the fortress gleamed like a jewel, its walls shimmering as if forged from purest gold atop a mountain of polished crystal.

label: he | try | ∅
sentence: He tried to climb but the mountain’s smooth glass repelled him, and he fell again and again.

label: he | build | hut
sentence: Resigned, he built a simple hut by its base and watched the princess drive her carriage daily around her radiant home.

label: day | turn | season
sentence: Days turned to seasons, and one afternoon, he witnessed three brigands quarrelling fiercely nearby.

label: he | call | ∅
sentence: “Peace be upon you,” he called.

label: they | pause | ∅
sentence: They paused and looked about but saw no one, resuming their fierce brawl.

label: he | repeat | ∅
sentence: “Peace be upon you,” he repeated twice more.

label: he | ask | cause
sentence: At last, stepping forth, he asked the cause of their strife.

label: one | say | ∅
sentence: “I possess a rod that opens any door with a single tap,” said one.

label: another | say | ∅
sentence: “I have a cloak that renders its wearer invisible,” said another.

label: third | declare | ∅
sentence: “My horse can leap over every pit and climb the glass mountain,” declared the third.

label: they | debate | ∅
sentence: They debated whether to share these gifts or part ways.

label: traveler | say | ∅
sentence: “I will trade you for all three,” said the traveler, “not with coins but with a treasure of greater worth.

label: ∅ | let | ∅
sentence: First, let me test these wonders.”

label: he | become | ∅
sentence: Mounting the horse, donning the cloak, and grasping the rod, he became unseen and struck the brigands decisively with the rod, sending them fleeing in defeat.

label: he | scale | peak
sentence: Now unseen and mounted on swift horse, he scaled the crystal peak.

label: door | swing | magic
sentence: At the fortress’s gate, locked tight, he tapped thrice with the rod; the door swung open as though by magic.

label: princess | sit | ∅
sentence: Inside, the princess sat alone, golden cup of wine before her.

label: he | slip | ring
sentence: Still cloaked in invisibility, he slipped her ring from his finger into the cup.

label: that | be | ring
sentence: “That is my ring!”

label: she | exclaim | ∅
sentence: she exclaimed, “and the one who bears it must be near to free me!”

label: she | search | fortress
sentence: She searched throughout the fortress but found no one.

label: traveler | throw | cloak
sentence: When she reached the gate, the traveler threw off his cloak and revealed himself.

label: joy | be | ∅
sentence: Her joy was radiant as sunshine.

label: he | embrace | she
sentence: He embraced her gently, and she kissed him sweetly, saying, “You have truly freed me.

label: we | celebrate | union
sentence: On the morrow, we shall celebrate our union under the stars.”

label: princess | return | form
sentence: Thus, the enchanted princess returned to her true form, and the traveler’s steadfast heart won both her freedom and her love in the timeless lands of Persia.
